settings.text: No demo · no mess
No contractor required
10× more durable

settings.heading: Cover Your Old Insulation. No Demo. No Mess. No Contractor Required.
settings.text: BlueTex installs directly over torn, sagging, stained insulation. Sheets are mechanically locked to the building at every purlin with aluminum TermBars — a clean, finished interior that lasts 10× longer than the original liner.
settings.image_alt: Finished BlueTex install with aluminum TermBars over old metal building insulation

settings.title: Vapor Barrier Fails
settings.text: The original white vinyl liner tears at fasteners, pulls away at seams, and stops sealing — usually within 5 to 10 years of install.

settings.title: Moisture Hits the Fiberglass
settings.text: Warm interior air carries moisture through the failed barrier and into the fiberglass blanket, which soaks it up like a sponge.

settings.title: Condensation on Cold Metal
settings.text: Moisture condenses against the cold roof and wall panels, drips back into the insulation, and leaves the sagging, stained mess you see today.

settings.heading: Why Old Insulation Goes Wet, Sagging, and Stained
settings.lead: Builder-grade vinyl vapor barriers fail within a few years. Once they tear or pull away from the purlins, warm interior moisture moves freely through the fiberglass and condenses on the cold metal roof and walls. The fiberglass soaks it up, sags under the weight, and leaves behind the brown stains and torn liner you see today.

settings.title: Goes Over the Old Stuff
settings.text: Install directly over torn or sagging insulation. No tearing out fiberglass, no dust, no cleanup.

settings.title: Mechanically Locked at Purlins
settings.text: Aluminum TermBars screw through BlueTex into every purlin, sealing the building edge-to-edge with no adhesives or staples.

settings.title: New Vapor Barrier and Finish In One
settings.text: BlueTex is the new finished interior surface — a continuous vapor barrier and a clean, washable white face.

settings.heading: Cover It With BlueTex and TermBars
settings.lead: BlueTex installs directly over the existing insulation — no demo, no fiberglass cleanup. Sheets are mechanically locked to the building at every purlin with aluminum TermBars, sealing the building behind a single continuous, washable surface.
settings.image_alt: Finished BlueTex install with aluminum TermBars

settings.title: Tear-Proof
settings.text: Reinforced film stands up to handling on a lift and stays intact under TermBar fasteners.

settings.title: Puncture-Resistant
settings.text: Holds up to ladders, tools, and incidental contact in working buildings.

settings.title: No Fumes, No Mess
settings.text: No spray foam, no off-gassing, no fiberglass cleanup. Building stays usable during install.

settings.title: Any Time of Year
settings.text: Install in summer or winter — performance is not weather-dependent.

settings.title: 10× More Durable
settings.text: Outlasts builder-grade vinyl liner by an order of magnitude under real-world conditions.

settings.title: Clean Finished Interior
settings.text: Finished white face wipes clean. No exposed fiberglass and no contractor required.

settings.heading: Built for Real Buildings, Real Crews, and Real Conditions
settings.text: BlueTex is engineered to handle what builder-grade insulation can't — and the install is straightforward enough for a small crew with basic tools.

settings.heading: Why Vapor Barriers Fail and What Wet Insulation Looks Like
settings.text: A short walk-through of what's happening inside the wall and roof — and why covering it with BlueTex stops the cycle.
